title: cynoglossum cheirifolium subvar. mariolense
intro: Cynoglossum cheirifolium subvar. mariolense is a subspecies of the Cynoglossum cheirifolium plant, a species of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). It is a perennial herb native to the Iberian Peninsula, and its name suggests a variant of the C. cheirifolium plant, though I can't confirm the differences between the two. The medicinal properties of Cynoglossum cheirifolium subvar. mariolense include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are derived from its alkaloid content. The plant is used to prepare herbal infusions and decoctions, which may help alleviate respiratory issues and promote overall well-being, although more research is needed to confirm its health benefits.
